definition: Japan is not a valid analogue for US sovereign debt sustainability. The US fails all five structural conditions that sustain Japan's 260% debt/GDP: positive NIIP (+70% vs -65%), domestic ownership (>90% vs ~70%), central bank ownership (>50% vs ~20%), current account surplus vs deficit, and deflationary backdrop vs above-target inflation.
evidence: US domestic Treasury ownership ~70% vs Japan's >90%
US runs persistent current account deficit vs Japan's surplus
US above-target inflation vs Japan's deflationary backdrop
open_questions: Does Japan's YCC exit experiment reveal whether the configuration survives removal of the deflation pillar?
